rationale: The citing article is an editor-period historical retrospective of JMR, while the cited paper provides a full 50-year empirical topical history of JMR; this is a clear thematic and scope complement rather than an unrelated reference. The citing note explicitly says it “extends prior histories… while complementing broader topical histories,” and the cited note matches that role (journal-wide topic/editor evolution), so this pair is plausible as a foundational/comparison source and should be reviewed in stage two.
